course_title: People Analytics: Theory, Strategy and Practical Application
course_description:
With the growth in digital technologies and data science comes a growing interest in people analytics within the HR profession. This course will help provide students with knowledge and expertise in this growing specialism; with a combination of theoretical and practical lecture/instruction, case study tutorials and hands on practical workshops, the course aims to help build and extend students' HR analytic competencies. The course will help to build skills in statistical analysis with a particular focus on application in HR related data. We will cover a range of quantitative HR analytic projects, such as: analyzing and predicting turnover as well as exploring drivers of employee engagement.

This course will helpﾠstudents understand some of the latest developments in the field of Human Resources, in particular the use of digital and analytic technologies in the management of people in organisations. The course will begin with contextualising HR analytics and its role in contributing to the HR function's offering to organisations. It will help explain and explore the use of people data in HR and how organisations are beginning to engage digital technologies and sophisticated analytic techniques to help make key HR related investment decisions. A key aim of the course is to ensure that students develop their analyticﾠcompetency, both in understanding how and why organisations can apply analytics to answer HR related business problems but also to understand some of the techniques used in HR analytics. With a focus on some of the key pillars of HR analytics: Employee Engagement, Employee Performance and Turnover the students will begin to explore analytic modelling potential. Importantly the course will enable students to consider some of the logistical, ethical and methodological challenges faced by HR analytics teams.ﾠ ﾠ ﾠﾠ

MGTS3609ﾠis one of the lastﾠcourses for students to take with the Human Resourcesﾠmajor in the Bachelor of Business (Management) degree.ﾠThe course will help students be aware of the lastest cutting-edge developments in the field of Human Resources.ﾠDigitial developments in HR are fast moving and this course will go some way to prepare students for the potential challenges that they may face (with regards to the analyses of people data) if they decide to follow a career in HR.ﾠ

course_aims: A main aim of the course in the HR major, is to help students obtain HR analytic competency and insight. By the end of the module the students will not only have an understanding of HR Analytics as a specialism, but they will also be able to conduct statistical analyses and interpret statistical output to help answer a theoretically informed business problem. They will have developed an understanding various models of the role that HR Analytics can play in organisations and have gained a critical understanding of HR analytics as an HR activity

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate an understanding of People/HR analytics as a field and the scope of projects that can be undertaken in an HR Analytics team.
Demonstrate an understanding of current theoretical and methodological approaches employed in HR analytics and understand the challenges in how these affect the way the knowledge base is interpreted.
Demonstrate an ability to conduct and interpret statistical analyses on HR related data, understand and report the importance of the findings (individually and as a team).
Show an understanding of the critical ethical dimension to people analytics as applied in practice, and demonstrate insight into managing the implications of ethical dilemmas.
Be able to critically evaluate the appropriate use of HR analytics projects.

assessment_details.title: Series of 4 Quizzes
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Longitudinal. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Students will be given a series of four quizzes (5% each) that test students' ongoing understanding of material from the course. Students will be quizzed on various features of the course, including tutorial activities, lecture material and video material (presented in the lecture and/or posted on blackboard). The quizzes will drawing questions randomly from a pool of questions and be open for 24 hours. These will be deployed to students on weeks 4,6,8 and 10. This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI). Students are advised that the use of AI technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Quizzes will be deployed on Blackboard.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .

assessment_details.title: Group Presentation
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Peer assessment factor. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Students will be assigned to groups and given an HR analytics case study with some analyses output (along with the associated dataset for information). The case study will be provided on blackboard. The groups will need to consider the case study, interpret the analyses, and present the importance of the findings to a hypothetical board of directors. The presentation will need to outline the findings, what implications the analyses has for the organisation and set out recommendations in terms of what the organisation should be considering (regarding investments in HR) going forward. The presentations will be recorded and all group members will need to take part in the presentation. The presentations will be presented during tutorials in weeks 11 and 12. Please note that you MUST be a member of a group in order to complete this group presentation task. Preliminary allocation to groups will take place in Week 5 and groups will be finalised by Week 6. Students who do not attend tutorials in Week 5 and do not notify course staff by the end of Week 6 with an adequate reason for their absence with appropriate documentation will not be allocated to a team and may receive zero for this assessment task. This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI). Students are advised that the use of AI technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submission will be via BB.
The presentations will be given during scheduled tutorials in Weeks 11 and 12.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .
assessment_details.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Invigilated exam
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This exam will present a case descripton of an analytics project with some analytics output. The students will be expected to interpret the output, report and summarise the findings along with an explanation of the implications that the findings have for HR decision making going forward. The students will also need to reflect on any ethical challenges that may arise from the project. Students will be given 120 minutes for the exam, under timed conditions. A mock exam will be provided to students before the revision session in the last week of semester. More details will be provided on Blackboard. This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI). Students are advised that the use of AI technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
This is an in-person exam. Exam papers will be collected at the end of the exam.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to defer this exam .

learning_activities.topic: Online Self Study Self-directed study - No Tutorial this week. Learning outcomes: L01, L02
Intro To People Analytics: HR in context How does HR Analytics as an offering fit into the broader sweep of HR activities and what does it bring to the field? Learning outcomes: L01, L02
HR Analytics Teams: Getting to know each other Learning outcomes: L01, L02
Intro To People Analytics: HR in context How do organisations arrange their HR Analytics teams and what kind of projects to they undertake? Learning outcomes: L01, L02
HR Analytics Teams: Working in groups Learning outcomes: L01, L02
Analytics HR and Technology What are the technological developments that we are seeing in HR that enable the full utilisation of HR Analytics? Learning outcomes: L01, L02
Analytic Activity Intro Learning outcomes: L01, L04
Engagement HR Analytics Why is employee engagement such an important focus of HR analytics teams? What is it? What analytic opportunities does engagement data enable? Learning outcomes: L02, L03
Employee Engagement Analyses Learning outcomes: L03
Turnover HR Analytics Why is turnover such an important part if HR analytics activities? Can we predict turnover and cost it? What opportunities does this provide for HR? Learning outcomes: L02, L03
Costing Turnover Learning outcomes: L03

Performance HR Analytics What do we mean by employee performance and what data might be useful as measures of performance? What are the limits to performance data and analytics? Learning outcomes: L02, L03
Performance Analyses Learning outcomes: L03

Diversity HR Analytics Why is HR analytics so important for the management of Diversity and inclusion in organisations? What kind of analytics might an organisation utilise with Diversity and Inclusion? Learning outcomes: L03, L04
Diversity Analyses Learning outcomes: L03
Workforce Planning What is work force planning and how does HR analytics help? Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
Automated Hiring Learning outcomes: L03

Practitioner Talk Talk from an HR analytics expert in a multinational organisation about the value add of HR analytics and a discussion of the use of machine learning in analytics Learning outcomes: L01, L04
In-Semester Break
A2 group work for presentations Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05

People Analytics - Stakeholder reporting It is one thing to be able to analyse and interpret HR data - but "so what"? What does it tell an organisation and how can you communicate the importance of the analyses? Reporting and convincing stakeholders of the importance of analytics is key. Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04

A2 Presentations A2 Presentations will occur live in your tutorials this week. King's Birthday Public Holiday - Monday 7 Oct 2024 - Check Blackboard for announcements about affected classes. Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05
Public Holiday - Please see Blackboard for details King's Birthday Public Holiday - Monday 7 Oct 2024 - Check Blackboard for announcements about affected classes. Learning outcomes: L01, L04
A2 Presentations A2 presentations will occur live in your tutorials this week. Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05

Ethical Considerations and Debates Just because you can analyse people data in the work place doesn't mean you should. What are some of the ethical challenges and opportunities of HR analytics? Learning outcomes: L04, L05
Self-directed exam revision SELF-DIRECTED REVISION - Please use your tutorial time this week to prepare for the exam. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
HR Analytics Scoping - Revision Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
